pr-classify.description: Classify the current project to pick the right GitHub protocol (OWNER full PR flow / FORK open PR and stop / READ_ONLY no commits no PR / NOT_GITHUB out of scope). Run at the start of every work session in a project directory; do not ask the user which case they are on.
pr-classify.template: Run `~/.config/opencode/scripts/gh-project-classify.sh $ARGUMENTS` and show the user the result. Branch on the exit code: 0=OWNER (full 10-step PR flow including squash-merge), 10=FORK (steps 1-8 only, do not auto-merge, hand off to upstream maintainer), 20=READ_ONLY (local edits only, no commits, no PRs, no issues), 30=NOT_GITHUB (follow the project's own rules), 40=NOT_GIT (do whatever the project asks). Use `--json` to parse the output. The script is the single source of truth for the project type; do not re-derive the case from `gh repo view` and ad-hoc bash.

pr-wait.description: Wait for a GitHub PR's CI and Copilot review, then continue the PR workflow.
pr-wait.template: Run `~/.config/opencode/scripts/gh-pr-wait.sh $ARGUMENTS` and read the verdict block. Follow the GitHub pull request workflow in AGENTS.md: if the script reports a real review with substantive comments, fetch them with `gh api /repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<N>/comments` and `gh pr view <N> --json reviews`, evaluate, and apply the valuable ones as additional commits (then re-run this command); if Copilot is quota-exhausted, leave a brief PR comment acknowledging it; if CI failed or Copilot requested changes, fix and push follow-up commits (then re-run); if green and approved (or quota-exhausted), squash-merge with `gh pr merge <N> --squash --delete-branch` and verify the linked issue is closed — but only if `gh-project-classify.sh` returned OWNER (exit 0). If it returned FORK (exit 10), stop after the PR is approved and hand the merge to the upstream maintainer; do NOT run `gh pr merge`. Do not re-implement the polling loop yourself; the script is the single source of truth for that.
